title: Storyboard — the Director's contract
description: One storyboard per video. The Director plans it; Voice, Visuals, Editor and Reviewer execute it beat-by-beat and never invent outside it. This is the 'brain' artifact: every downstream station is replaceable as long as it honors this contract.

lane.description: Content lane the Strategist picked this from.

name_anchor.description: Famous name/brand the hook is anchored to (Sam Altman, Claude, Google...). Empty if none applies — never force one.

framing_notes.description: How claims must be framed (e.g. 'reported consideration, not enacted policy'). Critic and Reviewer check the script against this.

sources.description: URLs every factual claim traces to.

concept.description: The visual through-line. Every beat's visual must serve it — one authored idea, not a slideshow of related clips.

metaphor.description: One continuous visual idea carrying the video (e.g. 'a cat performing at a grand piano as the hall turns digital').

escalation.description: How the imagery evolves across beats in parallel with the narration's stakes (playful -> ominous, small -> global...).

continuity.description: Exact recurring character/object/style description prepended verbatim to every generated-image prompt so identity stays consistent across shots.

negative_prompt.description: Global negative prompt for all generated images (deformed anatomy, inconsistent identity, embedded text, logos, watermarks...).

type.description: kinetic_text = word-by-word text hook. visual_spectacle = the concept's imagery is the scroll-stopper; text stays minimal.

text.description: 4-6 words, kinetic word-by-word buildable, no fabricated events.

variant_rank.description: Which of the 3 generated variants was chosen (by you or by the agent when hook_pick=auto).

beats.description: Ordered narrative beats. Target total spoken length 30-40s.

narration.description: Exact voiceover text for this beat.

emotion.description: Drives TTS delivery (speed/tags) and pacing. Never the same emotion twice in a row.

est_seconds.description: Director's estimate; Editor trues it up from the rendered audio.

music_intensity.description: Music arc per beat: 0 = bed only, 3 = peak. Should rise with the narration's stakes, not sit flat.

overlay.description: On-screen text chip for this beat, empty for none. Last beat's overlay is the CTA in Hook style (bookend rule B7).

source.description: broll_* = stock (Pexels). generated_image = FLUX local / Gemini ladder. face = presenter cam (clone/avatar later). screen_capture = product UI or repo page.

query.description: Stock-search query, tightly tied to the narration's concrete subject — not the abstract theme.

must_show.description: The one thing the frame must contain for the beat to make sense (proof rule B6).

layout.description: split_face_bottom = b-roll top / face bottom (the default split). Max 3 layers total incl. captions (rule B4).

camera.description: Slow Ken-Burns style move applied by the Editor.

transition_in.description: flash only at the hook->body cut (rule from creator teardown).

mood.description: Director picks from the curated royalty-free library in assets/music/<mood>/.

duck_under_voice.description: Loudness targets: narration ~-6dB peak, music -22 to -16dB under voice.

end_with_silence.description: Music stops on the final word; ~1s of silence so the CTA/question lands.

text.description: 3-5 punchy words, rendered as the final beat's overlay in Hook style.
